repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue PartialID entry gives a selected LLM-cascade cutoff endpoint; the closest local analogues are pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded-style bounded RD endpoint geometry and pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec selected-target bookkeeping, neither this selected cascade boundary value.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference covers fixed linear functionals over convex smoothness classes and KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD covers fixed-cutoff discrete running-variable honest inference; neither subsumes a cost-quality selected cascade cutoff. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1511.06028, https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.04086.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue PartialID entry states same-frontier non-identification of a selected local cascade value or a critical-curvature sign-certification index.

published_prior_art.cite: Kotte2026UCCI selects an isotonic calibrated threshold by constrained cost minimization and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt characterizes deterministic cascade frontier geometry; neither identifies or separates V_*. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.18796, https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06350.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives finite-atom active-set geometry for curvature-indexed selected endpoint bounds.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020SimpleHonest indexes honest intervals by smoothness budgets but does not give finite-atom selected-cutoff active-set geometry. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.01200.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo PartialID or Stat entry combines selected cascade cutoff inference with generated calibrated-score drift.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018BandwidthSnooping, BachocPreinerstorferSteinberger2016PostSelection, CalonicoCattaneoFarrell2018OptimalBandwidthRBC, DongKolesar2021RunningVariableMeasurementError, and Pernot2023IsotonicStratification cover adjacent selection, RD inference, generated-running-variable, or isotonic-mass issues, not this combined generated-score selected-threshold CI. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.0267, https://arxiv.org/abs/1611.01043, https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.00236, https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.07388, https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05180.

novelty_flags.label: proposal-level comparator bookkeeping
novelty_flags.one_line: The load-bearing drafter handoff reports comparator_promise_table as missing while the proposal names Kotte2026UCCI, Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt, ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference, KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD, and ZhangBenMichaelImai2022SafePolicyRD as comparators; reconcile the SC6 artifact/handoff before Stage 0.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:selected-sharp-set / main identification kernel
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Sharpness is conditional on the imposed range-respecting C^2 curvature-budget extension class, not derived from unrestricted observed-data primitives.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The selected endpoint interval is a field-tier sharp bound under the stated smooth-extension model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:postselect-honest-ci / generated-score drift handle
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The dense-regime inference rung is conditional on constructing the data-only hat G_n drift envelope and selected-threshold critical value.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage is only a conditional dense-regime open handle, not a settled theorem for atomic isotonic-score supports.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:quadratic-selection-growth / dense selected threshold
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Quadratic feasible-argmin growth is accepted as a conditional local regularity premise for the dense inference handle.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The post-selection CI conjecture is bound to laws with this isolated selected-cutoff regularity.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Reconcile the comparator_promise_table mismatch between the typed core and drafter handoff, with one row per named comparator and a statement-level tighten, separate, reduce, or background relation.
Add a worked finite-atom example computing L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M), and M_*(P) from two or three score atoms so the critical-curvature object is auditable before proof search.
State an explicit exogenous-cutoff corollary recovering the Armstrong-Kolesar and Kolesar-Rothe fixed-cutoff endpoint logic, including the accumulating-support collapse condition.
For conj:active-set-geometry, name the finite-dimensional optimization or interpolation route that produces the partition and the endpoint-attaining extremizers.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the mathematical kernel is field-new and well-posed, but the comparator-table handoff mismatch must be resolved before Stage 0.
